title: Palladius on Husbondrie: source lineage for CME00114
summary: CME00114 is a repository copy of the University of Michigan encoding of Mark Liddell's 1896 edition. Liddell directly transcribed the dedication manuscript now Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2; he used the later Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369 to correct and collate readings, and his appendix compares Barton Lodge's earlier EETS edition with that same Add. A. 369 witness. A complete 304-canvas Digital Bodleian facsimile of Duke Humfrey d. 2 is cached. The competing same-title three-canvas object is explicitly partial. No complete public page-image source for Add. A. 369 was found.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00114
entities.description: The checked-in XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00114; the file is 777,936 bytes.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00114
entities.description: Public electronic text containing Liddell's title page, the manuscript Tabula, prohemium, complete thirteen-book poem, appended collation Tabula, twelve-book list of variations, and two unnumbered errata pages. The printed dedication and Temporary Preface are not encoded.

entities.label: Palladius on Husbondrie (DIMEV 1071 with prohemium DIMEV 254)
alternate_labels: The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica
entities.description: The anonymous Middle English verse translation of Palladius's De re rustica, including the sixteen-stanza authorial prohemium and thirteen books. This abstract work layer is distinct from physical witnesses, scholarly editions, digital encodings, and manuscript-associated indices.

date_statements.label: Translation
date_statements.value: By 1444; probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript
date_statements.notes: The dedication-copy context supports a 1444 terminus ante quem, but the inspected evidence does not establish 1439 as an authorial terminus post quem.

entities.label: Palladius, De re rustica
entities.description: The late-antique Latin agricultural source work attributed to Rutilius Taurus Aemilianus Palladius. It is the source work translated into Middle English, not a manuscript witness or edition of the Middle English poem.

date_statements.label: Composition
date_statements.value: Fourth century according to the current Bodleian authority heading

entities.label: Mark Liddell, The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (1896)
entities.description: Liddell's edition transcribes the former Fitzwilliam manuscript now Duke Humfrey d. 2. Its Temporary Preface states that Add. A. 369 was used to correct the text where the base deviates from the original and that base readings are retained in notes. Printed pp. 259-289 append a systematic collation of Lodge's EETS edition with Add. A. 369. The title says Part I—Text; no later part is represented here.

bibliographic.citation: Mark Liddell, ed., The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (Berlin: E. Ebering, 1896).
bibliographic.title: The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2
alternate_labels: Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d.2
entities.description: The carefully produced dedication copy to Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester, and Liddell's direct base. The manuscript contains the alphabetical index at fols. x r-xviii v and the work proper at fols. xix r-118v, with the prohemium at xix r-xxi v and the main translation restarting at 1r. Current catalog evidence dates production to 1439-1444 and leaves authorship uncertain.

physical_description.format: Codex; usually 28 written lines with blank lines between seven-line stanzas; main hand combines anglicana and secretary, with a second scribe at fols. 54-61
notes: Prohemium and book envois use multicolored script; the final envoy also uses gold.
The prohemium opens at fol. xix r; Book I opens at fol. 1r with Humfrey's arms.
Liddell's legacy Arabic foliation must not be confused with current Digital Bodleian labels.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete 304-canvas facsimile of MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2
entities.description: The complete provider-published IIIF Presentation 2 object, including boards, endleaves, blanks, all current folio surfaces, and tail matter. The encoded Tabula is canvases 21-38; the cataloged work proper is canvases 39-280. This is the complete object, UUID 9e046a1c-7f55-457e-8098-15ff757c0ee7.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 304 color-image canvases

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected three-canvas object for MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2
entities.description: A competing same-title provider object containing only Upper board, fol. xix recto, and fol. 001r. It is useful for discovery but partial relative to the codex and must not be mistaken for the complete 304-canvas object.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with three selected color-image canvases

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Add. A. 369
entities.description: A 1440-1460 English parchment witness of the Middle English verse translation at fols. 14v-121v. It is Liddell's correction/collation witness and Lodge's direct base, not Liddell's base. It lacks a leaf in each of the first two quires, two leaves after fol. 92, part of fol. 121, the last 43 lines of Book XII, and all Book XIII.

holding.notes: Formerly at Colchester Castle; acquired by the Bodleian in 1886.

physical_description.format: Codex; target in seven-line rhyme-royal verse, imperfect through physical loss
notes: Target rubric: De preceptis rei rustice.
Target ends abruptly in Book XII; Book XIII is absent.

entities.label: Catalog-linked one-image Digital Bodleian object for MS. Add. A. 369
entities.description: The current manuscript catalog links a Digital Bodleian surrogate with exactly one canvas, fol. 009v. Because the target begins at fol. 14v, the object has zero overlap with the work and cannot support Liddell/Add. A. 369 collation checks.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with one 35mm-derived image, fol. 009v

entities.label: Barton Lodge, Palladius on Husbondrie, EETS Original Series 52 and 72 (1873, 1879)
entities.description: The earlier edition directly transcribed the then Colchester Castle manuscript now Add. A. 369. Barton Lodge edited the text; Sidney J. H. Herrtage edited the rhyme index. The direct title page governs over DIMEV's erroneous first name 'Burton.' Liddell used this edition in his appended comparison with Add. A. 369.

bibliographic.citation: Palladius on Husbondrie: From the Unique MS. of About 1420 A.D. in Colchester Castle, ed. Barton Lodge, with a Ryme Index ed. Sidney J. H. Herrtage, EETS Original Series 52 and 72 (London: N. Trübner, 1873 and 1879).
bibliographic.title: Palladius on Husbondrie
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 52 and 72
bibliographic.publisher: N. Trübner for the Early English Text Society

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Liddell's 1896 edition
entities.description: A 566-page provider PDF with searchable OCR and exact item metadata. The scan includes title, dedication, Temporary Preface, and printed pp. 1-289, with blank scan surfaces interleaved after many page pairs. It ends before the two unnumbered errata pages present in the U-M encoding.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete provider PDF, OCR text, and item metadata; the scanned exemplar lacks the encoded errata leaves

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Lodge and Herrtage's EETS edition
entities.description: A 432-page provider PDF containing the title, preliminaries, manuscript table, transcript through printed p. 220, notes, glossarial and rhyme indices, and corrigenda through printed p. 387.

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 records 1071 and 254
entities.description: Versioned scholarly records for the main Palladius poem (1071; IMEV/NIMEV 654) and its separately indexed prohemium (254), with witness and bibliography data. Record 1071 says twelve books, while the direct Liddell text and XML have thirteen labeled books; this discrepancy is preserved rather than silently harmonized.

agents.name: Barton Lodge
notes: The direct edition title says Barton; DIMEV v1.0 says Burton.

agents.name: Sidney J. H. Herrtage
notes: Editor of the rhyme index in EETS OS 72.

agents.name: Digital Index of Middle English Verse

rights.basis: The work is directly attested in a 1439-1444 manuscript and the official U-M item containing its text is marked Public Domain / No Copyright-United States. This statement concerns the medieval wording, not manuscript photographs, modern catalog data, or provider interfaces.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two principal Middle English witnesses and an earlier edition
editorial_practices.representation: Duke Humfrey d. 2 selected as direct text; Add. A. 369 used for correction and collation
editorial_practices.description: Liddell chose the former Fitzwilliam dedication manuscript as base because he believed it prepared for Humfrey from the author's copy. The later Add. A. 369 version is not silently substituted as base.
editorial_practices.scope: Tabula, prohemium, and complete thirteen-book text, printed pp. 1-258

editorial_practices.source_feature: Add. A. 369 readings and Lodge's EETS transcript
editorial_practices.representation: Corrections and footnotes in the main text plus appended Tabula and book-by-book variation lists
editorial_practices.description: Liddell says the later Bodleian version corrects places where the base departs from the original, while base readings are placed below; the appendix notes Add. A. 369 variants and Lodge misreadings.
notes: Exact line-level provenance remains to be checked against complete Add. A. 369 images.

editorial_practices.description: Liddell's Temporary Preface and appendix method statement assign abbreviation expansions to italic type and editorial supplies to square brackets.
editorial_practices.scope: Transcribed medieval text and collation apparatus

editorial_practices.description: Liddell explicitly accepts responsibility for punctuation; it must not be read as direct witness punctuation.
Liddell explicitly accepts responsibility for capital letters in the edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Liddell volume with dedication, Temporary Preface, text, apparatus, and errata
editorial_practices.representation: Title page, printed pp. 1-289, and errata encoded; dedication and Temporary Preface omitted
editorial_practices.description: The U-M item selects the textual edition and apparatus needed for the corpus rather than reproducing every modern preliminary leaf.

evidence.locator: TEMPHEAD, IDG, seven DIV1 source layers and complete descendants
evidence.summary: Direct local evidence for repository identity, U-M handle, exact edition title, source-layer selection, page milestones, Tabula, prohemium, thirteen books, collation, variations, notes, and errata.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural, page-boundary, and witness-crosswalk audit of CME00114.xml.
evidence.locator: Element counts, DIV hierarchy, LG-length distribution, Book XIII TABLE, PB REF 4 and 10-300, first and final lines
evidence.summary: Confirms 777,936 bytes; seven DIV1, 66 DIV2, 227 DIV3, 934 LG, 6,605 L, 292 PB, 2,015 NOTE, nineteen P, 1,002 ITEM, one eight-row TABLE, thirteen book headings, and exact Liddell printed-page coverage. The ITEM total comprises 499 manuscript-Tabula, 464 collation-Tabula, 38 errata, and one TEMPHEAD revision-description ITEM. The scan comparison confirms that the cached exemplar lacks PB REF 299-300 errata.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00114.
evidence.summary: Official current item evidence for title, Palladius and Liddell contributor display, Berlin 1896 publication, full content menu, public access, and the item's Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement. Both item URLs returned HTTP 403 to unauthenticated command-line GET requests during the final link audit, so content and structure claims are also independently grounded in the checked-in XML and scanned edition.

evidence.citation: Mark Liddell, ed., The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (Berlin: E. Ebering, 1896).
evidence.locator: title PDF p. 7; Temporary Preface PDF pp. 11-12; printed pp. 1-289 on nonblank surfaces PDF pp. 15-561
evidence.summary: Immediate-edition evidence for exact title and editor, direct Duke Humfrey d. 2 base, Add. A. 369 correction/collation, editorial expansions, supplies, capitalization and punctuation, complete poem, and appended comparison. The cached provider scan ends at printed p. 289 and lacks the encoded errata leaves.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for middleenglishtra00pall.
evidence.locator: metadata object and exact PDF/OCR file inventory
evidence.summary: Confirms the item identifier, 570 source images, exact PDF and OCR filenames, byte sizes, hashes, UBC source, and public download. It says 'Marc Harvey' rather than the title page's Mark and contains no inspected rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2.
evidence.summary: Current official record for exact shelfmark, contents and loci, Middle English language, 1439-1444 date, England origin, physical features, hands, decoration, dedication-copy context, provenance, and uncertain authorship. The record was created 26 March 2026 from David Rundle's description edited by Andrew Dunning.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian complete IIIF object for MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, UUID 9e046a1c-7f55-457e-8098-15ff757c0ee7.
evidence.locator: 304 canvases; fol. x r canvas 21; fol. xviii v canvas 38; fol. xix r canvas 39; fol. 1r canvas 45; fol. 118v canvas 280; fol. 119r canvas 281
evidence.summary: Complete object and visual boundary evidence. Fol. x r begins the Tabula; xviii v ends it with Explicit tabula; xix r opens the decorated prohemium; 1r opens Book I; 118v contains the colored final envoy and explicit; 119r is blank. Manifest attribution applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the images.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian selected-image IIIF object for MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, UUID a4593fe2-94d4-4f3e-9161-d2080bb35471.
evidence.locator: Exactly three canvases: Upper board, fol. xix recto, fol. 001r
evidence.summary: Confirms that this prominent same-title search result is a partial three-canvas selection, not the complete codex. It carries the same Bodleian photo attribution and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Add. A. 369.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for exact shelfmark and ARK, target title/rubric/incipit/explicit, Middle English rhyme-royal form, 1440-1460 English production, physical losses, Colchester provenance, Lodge edition, 1886 Bodleian acquisition, and a reported one-image digital surrogate.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian one-image IIIF object for MS. Add. A. 369, UUID 61120deb-7b6b-409d-bd8d-461dc3432a00.
evidence.summary: The complete published object and exact manifest directly show one 35mm-derived image at fol. 009v, before the target begins at fol. 14v, so target overlap is zero. The exact manifest attribution applies CC BY-NC 4.0; the cached ZIP contains the manifest, inventory, and sole 1600-pixel image.

evidence.citation: Barton Lodge, ed., Palladius on Husbondrie, with rhyme index ed. Sidney J. H. Herrtage, EETS Original Series 52 and 72 (1873, 1879).
evidence.locator: title PDF p. 7; preface and table PDF pp. 9-40; transcript printed pp. 1-220 / PDF pp. 41-260; apparatus through printed p. 387 / PDF p. 427
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for Barton Lodge's name, Add. A. 369 as the former Colchester Castle manuscript, exact edition boundaries, physical gaps that remove the end of Book XII and all Book XIII, and Sidney Herrtage's rhyme-index role.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 1071 and 254 with manuscript and bibliography data.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml records 1071 and 254; data/MSSIndex.xml Duke Humfrey d.2 and Add. A.369; data/Bibliography.xml Liddell1896, Lodge1872-79, Howlett1977
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for preferred title, normalized incipits, IMEV/NIMEV 654, rhyme schemes, agriculture subject, component indexing, witness census and loci, and editions. The release is CC BY 4.0. Direct sources correct its Burton/Barton error and preserve its twelve-versus-thirteen-book discrepancy.

Current contextual provider terms linked from the exact Duke Humfrey d. 2 manifest attributions. The exact manifests state CC BY-NC 4.0; commercial or otherwise out-of-license uses require separate permission.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, using Special Collections, ordering material, remote copying, Imaging Services, and image-reproduction guidance.
evidence.summary: Current official access routes: unrestricted Group A reader registration for on-site Special Collections use at the Weston Library; request-system ordering; paid reference scans or Imaging Services; and separate permission/reproduction conditions for supplied images.

open_questions.question: Which individual Liddell readings come directly from Duke Humfrey d. 2, from Add. A. 369 correction, from editorial conjecture, or from later errata?
next_steps: Compare every printed reading and note against the complete cached Duke Humfrey d. 2 images.
Obtain complete Add. A. 369 images and classify each B reading rather than treating all departures from the base alike.
Apply the thirty-eight errata before drawing conclusions about Liddell's final intended text.

open_questions.question: Which Liddell exemplar supplied the two unnumbered errata pages encoded as PB REF 299-300?
next_steps: Locate another complete physical or digital Liddell copy with leaves after printed p. 289.
Image and compare both errata pages against the thirty-eight XML ITEM elements.
Keep completeness of the cached provider scan separate from completeness of the represented edition's leaf set.

open_questions.question: Can a complete facsimile of MS. Add. A. 369, especially fols. 14v-121v, be obtained with exact research and reuse terms?
next_steps: Do not count the public one-image object as target coverage: its sole canvas is fol. 009v.
Ask Bodleian Special Collections about existing photography or microfilm and request a complete target run through Imaging Services if necessary.
Obtain order-specific research, repository, illustration, and commercial reuse terms before redistributing supplied images.
notes: No complete public target facsimile was found in the checked scope; this does not establish nondigitization.

review_notes: The base-versus-collation distinction is explicit throughout: Duke Humfrey d. 2 is Liddell's direct base; Add. A. 369 supplies corrections and collation; Lodge's EETS edition is an additional appendix comparator.
Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369 is the former Colchester Castle manuscript. It is not a British Library manuscript, and the historical location is not treated as the current holding.
The current d.2 foliation is not a continuous x r-118v run: index x r-xviii v, prohemium xix r-xxi v, then main text 1r-118v. Liddell's legacy foliation remains edition evidence only.
The full d.2 object is UUID 9e046a1c-7f55-457e-8098-15ff757c0ee7 with 304 canvases. Same-title UUID a4593fe2-94d4-4f3e-9161-d2080bb35471 has only three selected canvases and is never called complete.

The complete cached Liddell provider PDF is complete for that scanned exemplar but does not include the U-M encoding's two unnumbered errata pages. Container completeness and edition-leaf coverage are therefore kept separate.

Rights remain layer-specific: U-M asserts NoC-US for its electronic item; exact Duke Humfrey d. 2 images and the public Add. A. 369 fol. 009v image are CC BY-NC 4.0, but that one Add. A. 369 image has zero target overlap; DIMEV v1.0 is CC BY 4.0; the inspected Internet Archive metadata provides no exact item license; any future complete or commissioned Add. A. 369 images require their own order-specific terms.
The generated dist/CME00114.pdf was inspected only as a repository viewer derivative and is not promoted into the historical source lineage.
